scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.

Q2_expl: Unfair punishment, ignores root causes.
Q3_expl: Misguided action, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive force, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Harmful, excessive, violates virtues.

Q1_expl: Failure to act due to dependency on others; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: No direct injustice, but unmet familial duty.
Q3_expl: Lacked courage to act independently despite adversity.
Q4_expl: Neutral; no excess or deficiency in desires.
moral_expl: Deficiencies in prudence and fortitude lower moral acceptability.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
Q1_expl: Balances saving four lives with risk, practical but involves sacrifice.
Q2_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one life; prioritizes quantity over individual rights.
Q3_expl: Courageous decision under pressure to save lives despite moral cost.
Q4_expl: Action is necessity-driven, not linked to moderation or excess.
moral_expl: Utilitarian choice saves more but violates fairness; mixed morality.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
Q1_expl: Sneaking out shows poor judgment, lacking practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Deception undermines fairness in family relationships.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
Q1_expl: Intended to help but lacked transparency; practical compromise.
Q2_expl: Deception undermines fairness; violates trust.
Q3_expl: Acted to aid friend but avoided truth-telling challenge.
Q4_expl: No excess or indulgence; neutral regarding moderation.
moral_expl: Good intent but dishonesty reduces moral acceptability.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
Q1_expl: Balancing saving three over one shows practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Prioritizing three lives over one raises fairness questions.
Q3_expl: Acting decisively despite moral conflict shows courage.
Q4_expl: No excess or indulgence; action is calculated.
moral_expl: Saves more lives but sacrifices one, net positive.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
Q1_expl: Sacrificing one to save others balances survival with moral compromise.
Q2_expl: Unjustly killing an innocent violates fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: Lacks courage to resist immoral demand; prioritizes survival over moral conviction.
Q4_expl: Extreme action under duress, not moderation or self-control.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.

scenario: Person X left their long-term band as soon as an opportunity came up to play with a more successful band.
Q1_expl: Opportunistic move, practical but may lack loyalty consideration.
Q2_expl: Leaving may disrupt fairness to original band; unclear obligations.
Q3_expl: Courage to pursue success, but avoids prior commitments.
Q4_expl: Prioritizes ambition over moderation in commitments.
moral_expl: Mixed virtues: prudent/courageous but lacks temperance/justice balance.

scenario: You are in charge of an expedition which is crossing the Sahara. A sand storm causes your jeep to swerve off the road and overturn in the middle of the desert. You know that the nearest oasis is a week’s walk away. There are eight of you and a very limited amount of water, which will not allow you all to survive the scorching heat.You shoot two people so that there will be enough water for you and the rest of the group. These two will die, but you and the other five will be able to survive.
Q1_expl: Action prioritizes survival but neglects moral balance; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfairly sacrifices two without consent; violates moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: Avoids collective adversity through violence, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Extreme action, no moderation in preserving life.
moral_expl: Violates core virtues; immoral despite survival intent.

scenario: Person X was able to convince their boss that they were ready for a promotion at work.
Q1_expl: Advocating for promotion requires practical wisdom to assess readiness and present case effectively.
Q2_expl: Promotion based on merit aligns with fairness, assuming equitable evaluation.
Q3_expl: Asserting oneself for advancement demonstrates courage in facing potential rejection.
Q4_expl: Self-promotion balanced with humility reflects moderate self-control.
moral_expl: Action aligns with virtues moderately, lacking excess or deficiency.

scenario: You see a woman getting hired only because her father is close friends with the boss.
Q1_expl: Nepotism ignores merit, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair hiring undermines meritocracy and fairness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage shown; favors convenience.
Q4_expl: Indulges favoritism, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Unjust, imprudent, intemperate; highly immoral.

scenario: Stole into a parking place while someone else was backing in
Q1_expl: Impulsive action, lacks foresight.
Q2_expl: Unfairly takes another's opportunity.
Q3_expl: No moral courage shown.
Q4_expl: Acted on selfish impulse.
moral_expl: Unfair, imprudent, lacks self-control.

scenario: A few years ago, I was single and I went to see a friend of mine in the Bahamas. I was extremely attracted to a man I met there who was married. I danced as close to the fire as I could, but I did nothing.
Q1_expl: Showed restraint but risked temptation; partial prudence.
Q2_expl: Flirting with a married person risks unfairness.
Q3_expl: Resisted acting, showing moral courage.
Q4_expl: Moderate self-control despite attraction.
moral_expl: Mixed virtues: restraint but risked injustice.

scenario: As I was backing out of a parking lot I bumped a parked car and left a minor dent. I didn't even feel the impact when I hit the car but it left a little bit of damage. I drove away without leaving a message or trying to contact the person.
Q1_expl: Failing to take responsibility shows lack of practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Avoiding accountability violates fairness and societal duty.
Q3_expl: Fleeing demonstrates cowardice, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Leaving reflects impulsivity, not self-control.
moral_expl: Unethical evasion of responsibility; harms others.

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
Q1_expl: Balances saving lives with harm, practical but morally complex.
Q2_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one for others, violating individual rights.
Q3_expl: Requires courage to act decisively under moral pressure.
Q4_expl: No excess or indulgence; action is deliberate, not impulsive.
moral_expl: Utilitarian benefit vs. moral violation; ethically ambiguous outcome.

scenario: Person X went to a fancy restaurant but couldn't pronounce the items on the menu.
Q1_expl: No moral decision involved; neutral action.
Q2_expl: No fairness or societal impact; neutral.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; neutral.
Q4_expl: No indulgence or restraint; neutral.
moral_expl: No moral extremes; neutral scenario.

scenario: Recently I was supposed to go to a meeting with a whole bunch of people who are not my friends, just acquaintances. I really didn't want to go. So I emailed the group saying that I had to work that day, when in reality I didn't.
Q1_expl: Lying avoids discomfort but lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Dishonesty undermines fairness and trust.
Q3_expl: Avoiding the meeting shows lack of courage.
Q4_expl: Prioritizing convenience over self-control.
moral_expl: Dishonest, unfair, and lacking courage or moderation.

scenario: You are in the head office of your bank together with four other people. Suddenly, the director calls you because he has discovered a bomb in an office on the ground floor. He knows you are a bomb disposal expert and asks you to defuse it. You realise immediately that there is not enough time to evacuate the people in the bank before the bomb explodes.You throw the bomb into the basement where there is the security vault. You know that the explosion will kill the security guard in the vault, but you and the other four people will be saved.
Q1_expl: Balanced saving lives vs. sacrifice, but lacks full moral consideration.
Q2_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one life without consent, violating fairness.
Q3_expl: Courageous action under pressure to prevent greater harm.
Q4_expl: Action was calculated, not impulsive, but context limits moderation.
moral_expl: Saves more lives but violates justice; morally ambiguous.

scenario: You are driving your new and very expensive car along a narrow country road. On the edge of the road you see a person who has just fallen off their bicycle. This person has hit their head violently and asks you if you can accompany them to the nearest hospital. You realise that because of the fall the person is now covered with mud.You leave the injured person on the side of the road. You know that sooner or later somebody will pass by and, in this way, you will not dirty the leather seats in your new car.
Q1_expl: Prioritizing car cleanliness over aiding an injured person lacks practical moral wisdom.
Q2_expl: Abandoning a vulnerable person violates fairness and societal moral duty.
Q3_expl: Avoiding discomfort to protect luxury shows cowardice, not courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive attachment to material comfort opposes moderation.
moral_expl: Gross neglect of human welfare for trivial reasons.

scenario: You see a boy placing a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
Q1_expl: Action is reckless, harmful; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair, violates others' well-being.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; malicious intent.
Q4_expl: Impulsive, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Harmful, violates all virtues.

scenario: You see a man telling his girlfriend that she must convert to his religion.
Q1_expl: Coercion lacks practical wisdom; not balanced.
Q2_expl: Unfair imposition; undermines autonomy.
Q3_expl: Misapplied strength, not virtuous courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive control, lacks moderation.
moral_expl: Coercive, disrespectful; violates multiple virtues.

scenario: Ate all the food before anyone else could have any
Q1_expl: No consideration for others' needs, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfairly deprives others of food, violates fairness.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; action lacks moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive indulgence, no self-control.
moral_expl: Severe deficiency in justice, temperance; highly immoral.

scenario: You see a man staying inside his home with the shades drawn on a rare sunny day.
Q1_expl: Neutral action; no clear prudence or imprudence.
Q2_expl: No societal impact; unrelated to justice.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; neutral fortitude.
Q4_expl: Moderate self-control; avoids excess sun exposure.
moral_expl: Neutral across virtues; no moral extremes.

scenario: Accidentally wounds a young soldier when firing a warning shot.
Q1_expl: Accidental harm shows lack of foresight.
Q2_expl: Unintended harm undermines fairness.
Q3_expl: Acted to prevent escalation but failed.
Q4_expl: Excessive force despite intent.
moral_expl: Good intent but harmful outcome.

scenario: Left food out for stray dogs
Q1_expl: Compassionate act, may lack long-term planning.
Q2_expl: Promotes animal welfare, aligns with fairness.
Q3_expl: Requires empathy but minimal adversity.
Q4_expl: Moderate provision, avoids excess.
moral_expl: Balanced virtues, morally positive.

scenario: Person X scratched their neighbor's expensive car with a key, as he always parked it at the front of PersonX's house.
Q1_expl: Action is impulsive, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Damaging property violates fairness and rights.
Q3_expl: Destructive act, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Act driven by anger, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues, extremely immoral.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.
Q1_expl: Balances saving five lives vs. one; practical but morally complex.
Q2_expl: Sacrifices one unfairly; prioritizes utility over individual rights.
Q3_expl: Courage to act decisively despite moral weight of killing.
Q4_expl: Extreme action (killing) lacks moderation in preserving life.
moral_expl: Saves lives but violates justice and temperance; morally conflicted.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to become a commercial airline pilot like him.
Q1_expl: Forcing career choice lacks practical wisdom balance.
Q2_expl: Ignores son's autonomy; unfair imposition.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; lacks moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive control, lacks moderation.
moral_expl: Unfair imposition, deficient in virtues.

scenario: Someone yells at a server at a restaurant because their meal is late
Q1_expl: Yelling shows poor judgment, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair treatment, violates moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: Aggression, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control, excessive anger.
moral_expl: Deficient in all virtues, harmful behavior.

scenario: I said some bad things about this guy that I work with. He told me some secrets that I promised not to share, but I did anyway. He didn't find out but I was afraid if he did, he might do something crazy.
Q1_expl: Broken promise shows poor judgment, lacks prudence.
Q2_expl: Betrayed trust, violated fairness and moral duty.
Q3_expl: Avoided accountability, lacked courage to uphold morals.
Q4_expl: Indulged in gossip, lacked self-control.
moral_expl: Severe violation of all virtues, highly immoral.

scenario: Person X punched a woman for wearing a hijab because PersonX thinks they should not be allowed in this country.
Q1_expl: Violence lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unjustly harms others' rights.
Q3_expl: Aggression, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: No self-control; violent excess.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues; extreme immorality.

scenario: When I was a football coach, I was often involved with the moms of the kids on the team. There were many mothers that were attractive and made themselves available. I was very tempted to have sex with one mother who came on to me.
Q1_expl: Succumbing to temptation lacks practical wisdom; prudence requires restraint.
Q2_expl: Breaches trust and fairness in a professional role; harms relationships.

Q4_expl: Lacks self-control; excessive desire undermines moderation.

scenario: Executes a squad of surrendering enemy troops.

Q2_expl: Violates fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: Shows cowardice, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive cruelty, no self-control.
moral_expl: Contrary to all virtues; extreme immorality.

scenario: Refuses to fire into a crowd to stop an enemy combatant.
Q1_expl: Prioritizes civilian safety over immediate threat, balancing moral duty.
Q2_expl: Upholds fairness by protecting innocent lives, risking societal harm.
Q3_expl: Courage to resist pressure, prioritizing ethical conviction.
Q4_expl: Restraint in avoiding reckless force, exercising self-control.
moral_expl: Moral courage and restraint outweigh potential risks.

scenario: Pulled his dog's tail
Q1_expl: Harmful action lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfairly inflicts harm on the dog.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; action is aggressive.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control and moderation.
moral_expl: Harmful, unjust, and intemperate action.

scenario: Beat up someone smaller than him
Q1_expl: Violence without justification lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unprovoked aggression violates fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: Bullying the weak opposes moral courage.
Q4_expl: Acting violently shows no self-control.
moral_expl: Extreme immorality across all virtues.

scenario: Refused to loan class notes to a friend who had been ill
Q1_expl: Refusing may lack practical wisdom in balancing help and responsibility.
Q2_expl: Unfair to deny aid to an ill friend without valid reason.

Q4_expl: Excessive self-interest over moderation in sharing.
moral_expl: Deficient in justice and temperance; slightly immoral.

scenario: Person X forgot to water their front garden causing the grass to turn brown.
Q1_expl: Forgetting to water reflects negligence, not prudent judgment.
Q2_expl: No direct impact on fairness or societal justice.
Q3_expl: No adversity or moral courage involved.
Q4_expl: Not related to moderation or self-control.
moral_expl: Minor neglect, not immoral but slightly irresponsible.

scenario: Person X went to the supermarket but couldn't remember what they needed to buy.
Q1_expl: Forgetting items lacks planning, not moral prudence.
Q2_expl: No impact on fairness or societal justice.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; no moral courage required.
Q4_expl: Forgetting does not relate to moderation or excess.
moral_expl: Neutral action; no moral valence in virtues.

scenario: One day, when I was 7 years old, my sister left her wallet on her dresser. I bumped the dresser and the wallet fell onto the floor. Many coins came out and I picked them up and put them in my pocket.
Q1_expl: Taking coins without permission lacks prudence.
Q2_expl: Theft violates fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage demonstrated; action avoids adversity.
Q4_expl: Succumbing to temptation shows lack of self-control.
moral_expl: Theft is unjust and lacks virtue.

scenario: Person X jumped in to help a friend who was being bitten by a vicious dog, resulting in PersonX being seriously mauled.
Q1_expl: Act shows courage but risks harm; some prudence in aiding friend.
Q2_expl: Upholds moral righteousness by protecting a friend from harm.
Q3_expl: Exemplifies extreme courage facing physical danger for moral good.
Q4_expl: Action unrelated to moderation of desires or pleasures.
moral_expl: Highly moral due to justice and fortitude, despite moderate prudence.

scenario: Broke a friend's phone and neither apologized or replaced it
Q1_expl: No effort to rectify mistake; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair to friend; neglects moral responsibility.
Q3_expl: Avoids accountability; lacks courage to act rightly.
Q4_expl: Failure to moderate selfishness; no self-control.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues; highly immoral.

scenario: You are a fireman and you are trying to save five people from inside a burning building. The only window from which the people can be evacuated is jammed and will not open. The fire will reach you in a short time. Outside on the window ledge of the floor below, there is a person who is waiting to be saved.With an axe you smash the window to get out. You know that when it falls, the heavy glass will kill the person on the lower floor, but you and the five people in danger will be able to escape.
Q1_expl: Balances saving lives with harm, but necessary in crisis.
Q2_expl: Sacrifices one unfairly; violates moral fairness.
Q3_expl: Courageous action under extreme pressure.
Q4_expl: No excess or indulgence; focused on survival.
moral_expl: Courageous but unjust, yielding mixed morality.

scenario: Laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny
Q1_expl: Polite social gesture, not deeply prudent.
Q2_expl: Neutral; maintains harmony but not fairness.
Q3_expl: Avoids conflict, lacks moral courage.
Q4_expl: Moderate insincerity, minor indulgence.
moral_expl: Minor social lie, acceptable but not virtuous.

scenario: You see a drunk elderly man offering to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
Q1_expl: Reckless, impaired judgment; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Disrupts social harmony; inappropriate propositioning.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; impulsive, not virtuous.
Q4_expl: Excessive indulgence; lacks self-control.

scenario: Person X offered to pay off the debts of their friend, who had been struggling to pay the bills since their partner died.

Q4_expl: Act is generous but not directly tied to moderation of desires.

scenario: Gave out toys to the Children's Hospital at Christmas
Q1_expl: Charitable act shows practical kindness, not complex moral balancing.

Q3_expl: No significant adversity faced; act is compassionate, not courageous.
Q4_expl: Generosity is moderate, not excessive or self-indulgent.
moral_expl: Highly virtuous, aligns with justice and temperance, lacks excess.

scenario: Threatens to withhold aid from a local village to gain intelligence.
Q1_expl: Coercion undermines practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Exploits vulnerability, violates fairness.
Q3_expl: Uses intimidation, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive force, lacks restraint.
moral_expl: Unjust, coercive, and immoderate action.

scenario: Lied to his girlfriend about not having other dates
Q1_expl: Lying undermines truthfulness; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Deception violates fairness in relationships.
Q3_expl: Avoiding honesty shows cowardice, not courage.
Q4_expl: Lying reflects lack of self-control.
moral_expl: Contrary to all virtues; highly immoral.

scenario: Worked all summer to give his parents a trip to Hawaii
Q1_expl: Balanced work and generosity prudently.
Q2_expl: Personal gift, neutral to societal fairness.
Q3_expl: Persevered through summer work challenges.
Q4_expl: Moderate saving for a meaningful purpose.
moral_expl: Virtues aligned positively, no excess/deficiency.

scenario: You see a girl taking all the Halloween candy from a bowl, leaving none for others.
Q1_expl: Action lacks practical wisdom, inconsiderate of others.
Q2_expl: Unfairly deprives others, violates fairness.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; greed, not courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive indulgence, no self-control.
moral_expl: Unjust, intemperate, imprudent; highly immoral.

scenario: Person X ate their lunch and went back to work with food stuck in their teeth.
Q1_expl: Minor oversight, not a moral choice.
Q2_expl: No impact on fairness or rights.
Q3_expl: No adversity or moral courage involved.
Q4_expl: Unrelated to moderation or self-control.
moral_expl: Neutral act with no moral weight.

scenario: You see a woman commenting out loud about how fat another woman looks in her jeans.
Q1_expl: Commenting on others' appearance lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Public shaming undermines fairness and dignity.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; action harms rather than upholds.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control and restraint in speech.
moral_expl: Action violates all virtues, highly immoral.

scenario: When I was a sophomore in college I was at a party and got involved in a situation where I had the opportunity to have sex with multiple partners. I had sex with all three of them in the same night. There were no emotional or physical repercussions after.
Q1_expl: Impulsive action lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Consensual but lacks moral righteousness focus.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; unrelated to courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive indulgence, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Deficient in prudence and temperance.

scenario: I used to work as a legal assistant. I lied to clients all of the time, the attorney I worked for lied all the time too. We just lie to our clients so things will work out the best for us.
Q1_expl: Lying for self-interest lacks prudence.
Q2_expl: Deceiving clients violates fairness and trust.
Q3_expl: Avoiding honesty shows moral cowardice.
Q4_expl: Habitual lying reflects lack of self-control.

Q3_expl: Fight reflects rashness, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control, driven by anger.
moral_expl: Deficient in all virtues, highly immoral.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
Q1_expl: Insensitive remark lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair comparison harms relationship fairness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage demonstrated; neutral.
Q4_expl: Immoderate speech lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Unkind, unfair, immoderate; moderately immoral.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
Q1_expl: Failure to plan or fulfill obligation.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
Q1_expl: Considerate act, balances generosity with means.

Q4_expl: Moderate expense, no excess indicated.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.

Q2_expl: Unfair harm, violates moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: No courage; aggression, not moral conviction.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control, excessive anger.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues, extremely immoral.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.

Q4_expl: Impulse to take without restraint; lacks moderation.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
Q1_expl: Neutral act; bake sale is practical but not strategic.

Q3_expl: Courage to act despite potential personal conflict.
Q4_expl: Moderate action; no excess or indulgence involved.
moral_expl: Balanced virtues, slightly positive due to fairness and courage.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.

Q3_expl: No moral adversity faced; action lacks courage or moral challenge.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.

Q4_expl: Cheating indulges dishonesty, lacks self-control.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.

Q2_expl: Sacrifices two lives unfairly; violates distributive justice.
Q3_expl: Acts courageously to save some despite risk.
Q4_expl: Irrelevant to moderation in desires/pleasures.
moral_expl: Prudent and brave but unjust, net harm remains.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
Q1_expl: No moral judgment; neutral action.
Q2_expl: No impact on fairness or rights.
Q3_expl: No adversity or moral challenge involved.
Q4_expl: Neglect may hint at minor deficiency in moderation.
moral_expl: Neutral or trivial moral significance overall.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
Q1_expl: Refusing lacks moral-practical balance.
Q2_expl: Denying fairness to vulnerable person.
Q3_expl: Avoiding moral duty shows cowardice.
Q4_expl: No excess indulgence involved.
moral_expl: Fails prudence, justice, fortitude.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
Q1_expl: Action lacks practical wisdom, disregards societal norms.
Q2_expl: Violates ethical treatment of the deceased, unfair.
Q3_expl: Misguided emotional response, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive, lacks self-control and moderation.
moral_expl: Severe violation of virtues, highly immoral.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
Q1_expl: Balances saving five versus harming one; practical but morally complex.
Q2_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one life; violates individual rights for greater good.
Q3_expl: Courageous to act decisively despite moral dilemma.
Q4_expl: No excess or indulgence; action is necessity-driven.
moral_expl: Saves more but violates justice; net positive yet ethically conflicted.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
Q1_expl: Vandalism lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Defacing public property undermines fairness.
Q3_expl: Act shows courage for a cause.
Q4_expl: Destructive act lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Immoral due to injustice, but some courage.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
Q1_expl: Booing lacks practical wisdom; imprudent for role.
Q2_expl: Undermines fairness and team spirit; unjust.
Q3_expl: No moral courage shown; contrary to fortitude.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control; excessive negativity.
moral_expl: Deficient in all virtues; immoral action.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
Q1_expl: Forgetting shows lack of foresight, opposite of prudence.
Q2_expl: Negligence harms others but isn't directly unjust.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; failure to act responsibly.
Q4_expl: No indulgence; carelessness, not lack of self-control.
moral_expl: Negligence caused harm; moderately immoral.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
Q1_expl: Balances saving lives with risk; practical but involves harm.
Q2_expl: Unfair to client, but prioritizes greater good; moral conflict.
Q3_expl: Demonstrates courage to act decisively in crisis.
Q4_expl: Action is neutral; not about moderation of desires.
moral_expl: Moral trade-off: saves three but sacrifices one.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
Q1_expl: Stealing lacks practical wisdom, contrary to prudence.
Q2_expl: Theft violates fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; action driven by selfish desire.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control, indulging in social approval.
moral_expl: All virtues deficient; action is highly immoral.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.
Q1_expl: Protecting partner shows practical wisdom in crisis.
Q2_expl: Action is about care, not fairness or societal justice.
Q3_expl: Exemplifies courage in facing immediate danger.
Q4_expl: Unrelated to moderation of desires or pleasures.
moral_expl: Heroic fortitude outweighs neutral virtues, highly moral.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
Q1_expl: Sacrificing assets for community good shows prudent balance.
Q2_expl: Addresses inequality, promotes fairness.
Q3_expl: Demonstrates courage in facing personal loss.
Q4_expl: Extreme sacrifice may lack moderation.
moral_expl: High prudence, justice, fortitude outweigh moderate temperance.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
Q1_expl: Theft lacks practical wisdom, acting impulsively without moral balance.
Q2_expl: Stealing violates fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: No courage to resist temptation; succumbs to adversity.
Q4_expl: Fails to exercise self-control over desires.
moral_expl: Action violates all virtues, highly immoral.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
Q1_expl: Used knowledge to win; neutral prudence.
Q2_expl: Fair participation; collaboration likely allowed.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity faced; neutral fortitude.
Q4_expl: Moderate effort; neutral temperance.
moral_expl: Just action with neutral other virtues.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
Q1_expl: Remembering orders shows practical wisdom but lacks moral complexity.
Q2_expl: Promotes fairness by valuing coworkers' preferences, fostering goodwill.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity; minimal courage required.
Q4_expl: Focuses on memory, not moderation of desires.
moral_expl: Positively impacts relationships but lacks deep moral weight.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
Q1_expl: Practical solution after failed repair attempt.
Q2_expl: No direct impact on fairness or societal relationships.
Q3_expl: Attempted repair shows effort, not exceptional courage.
Q4_expl: Moderate action; replaced only necessary item.
moral_expl: Prudent and temperate, but morally neutral overall.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
Q1_expl: Lack of boundaries led to exploitation, not prudent.
Q2_expl: Unfair burden without gratitude or compensation.
Q3_expl: Helped but failed to uphold self-respect.
Q4_expl: Overextended without self-control to refuse excess.
moral_expl: Exploitation and imbalance tilt toward immorality.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
Q1_expl: Addresses urgent needs with practical aid, balancing care and resources.
Q2_expl: Promotes fairness by aiding vulnerable individuals in crisis.
Q3_expl: Requires resolve to act amid adversity, supporting moral duty.
Q4_expl: Neutral; focuses on aid, not moderation of desires.
moral_expl: Strongly aligns with prudence, justice, and fortitude.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
Q1_expl: Sacrificing a life to save others is pragmatic but violates moral principles.
Q2_expl: Unjustly sacrificing one without consent undermines fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: Acting decisively in crisis shows courage but lacks moral conviction in valuing life.
Q4_expl: Extreme action without restraint contradicts moderation and self-control.
moral_expl: Pragmatic but unjust, violating key virtues; morally ambiguous yet leaning negative.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
Q1_expl: Balancing fear with altruism shows practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Promotes societal welfare through selfless contribution.
Q3_expl: Overcoming fear consistently demonstrates courage.
Q4_expl: Moderate frequency, not excessive or deficient.
moral_expl: High fortitude and justice outweigh neutral temperance.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
Q1_expl: Helping child see parade is considerate, not requiring complex moral balance.
Q2_expl: Promotes fairness by enabling participation; no injustice involved.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity or moral courage required.
Q4_expl: Action unrelated to moderation of desires or pleasures.
moral_expl: Positively moral due to kindness and fairness, but not exceptional.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
Q1_expl: Avoiding payment lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfairly deprives theater of revenue.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; avoids rightful cost.
Q4_expl: Indulges desire without restraint.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues; clearly immoral.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
Q1_expl: Deceitful gain lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfairly claiming unearned reward.
Q3_expl: No adversity faced; avoids responsibility.
Q4_expl: Indulges in undeserved gain.
moral_expl: Unjust and imprudent, moderately immoral.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
Q1_expl: Deception lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfairly manipulates game integrity.
Q3_expl: Avoids honest challenge; cowardice.
Q4_expl: Indulges in dishonest gain.
moral_expl: Deception undermines all virtues.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
Q1_expl: Action lacked moral wisdom, impulsive.
Q2_expl: Racial slur violates fairness, harms relationships.
Q3_expl: No courage; action upheld prejudice, not morals.
Q4_expl: No self-control; indulged in harmful behavior.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues, highly immoral.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
Q1_expl: Swearing lacks practical wisdom; impulsive, not prudent.
Q2_expl: Unfairly harms cashier; violates moral fairness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; aggression, not fortitude.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control; excessive anger.
moral_expl: Deficient in all virtues; highly immoral.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
Q1_expl: Reckless, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Violates social fairness and rights.
Q3_expl: No moral courage demonstrated.
Q4_expl: Impulsive, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Contrary to three virtues, neutral on one.

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.
Q1_expl: Lifting in business clothes is impractical, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: No direct impact on fairness or societal relationships.
Q3_expl: Shows physical effort but not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Inappropriate attire suggests lack of situational moderation.
moral_expl: Neutral overall; odd but not immoral.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
Q1_expl: Secret voting avoids conflict but lacks honesty, balancing poorly.
Q2_expl: Deceit undermines fairness in marital support.
Q3_expl: Lacks courage to openly express opinion.
Q4_expl: Secrecy reflects lack of self-control in honesty.
moral_expl: Deceptive and unfair, but not severely immoral.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
Q1_expl: Promotes inclusion wisely, balancing social dynamics.
Q2_expl: Fosters fairness by countering exclusion.
Q3_expl: Requires courage to defy social neglect.
Q4_expl: Neutral; unrelated to moderation of desires.
moral_expl: Encourages fairness and courage, morally positive.

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
Q1_expl: Avoiding conflict shows practical wisdom but neglects family duty.
Q2_expl: Avoidance may undermine fairness in family relationships.
Q3_expl: Prioritizing personal peace over obligation shows partial courage.
Q4_expl: Avoidance lacks moderation in addressing family tensions.
moral_expl: Self-care over duty, moderately acceptable but unbalanced.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.
Q1_expl: Prioritizes immediate pleasure over long-term consequences; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Consensual exchange, but transactional intimacy may undermine relational fairness.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity faced; action aligns with environment, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Indulges desires without restraint; lacks moderation in pleasures.
moral_expl: Deficient in prudence, fortitude, and temperance; neutral in justice. Overall slightly immoral.

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.
Q1_expl: Bias undermines judgment; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair treatment violates moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: No courage; succumbs to personal bias.
Q4_expl: Indulges dislike; lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues; extremely immoral.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
Q1_expl: Failure to act wisely in crisis.
Q2_expl: Neglects moral duty to others.
Q3_expl: Lacks courage to act morally.
Q4_expl: Inaction unrelated to moderation.
moral_expl: Gross neglect of moral virtues.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
Q1_expl: Pressure may override practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair imposition on daughter's autonomy.
Q3_expl: Misplaced persistence, not moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive ambition, lacks moderation.
moral_expl: Excess in ambition, deficiency in fairness.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
Q1_expl: Action prioritizes saving more lives but violates moral balance by sacrificing innocents.
Q2_expl: Unjustly denies two people fairness; their lives are forcibly traded without consent.
Q3_expl: Requires courage to act decisively, but violates moral conviction against killing.
Q4_expl: Extreme action lacks moderation; directly causes harm instead of seeking alternatives.
moral_expl: Saves more but violates justice and temperance; morally conflicted outcome.

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
Q1_expl: Offering temporary help balances compassion with practicality.
Q2_expl: Supports fairness by aiding someone in need.
Q3_expl: Requires courage to take responsibility.
Q4_expl: Limited timeframe shows moderation.
moral_expl: Balanced, compassionate, and moderate action.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.
Q1_expl: Forgetting passport shows lack of foresight, contrary to prudence.
Q2_expl: No direct impact on fairness or societal relationships.
Q3_expl: Mistake does not involve courage or adversity.
Q4_expl: No indulgence or lack of self-control involved.
moral_expl: Primary moral failing is imprudence, but not severe.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
Q1_expl: Cheating shows poor judgment, lacking practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair, violates trust and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: Avoiding moral challenges, lacks courage.
Q4_expl: Indulges desires without restraint.
moral_expl: Extreme immorality across all virtues.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
Q1_expl: Leaving avoids responsibility; lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfairly shifts financial burden; violates fairness.
Q3_expl: Fails to uphold moral duty; lacks courage.
Q4_expl: Impulsive escape; no self-control.
moral_expl: Harmful, dishonest, neglects all virtues.

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
Q1_expl: Helps avoid penalty, practical kindness.
Q2_expl: Aids fairness but may bypass rules.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity faced.
Q4_expl: Moderate, selfless act without excess.
moral_expl: Positive intent, minor ethical conflict.

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
Q1_expl: Action saves many but sacrifices one; practical yet morally complex.
Q2_expl: Unjust to sacrifice innocent technician; fairness compromised.
Q3_expl: Courageous act to protect lives despite risks.
Q4_expl: Extreme action lacks moderation; intentional harm.
moral_expl: Saves lives but violates justice and temperance; mixed morality.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
Q1_expl: Solving a crossword is neutral; no moral principles involved.
Q2_expl: No direct impact on fairness or societal relationships.
Q3_expl: No adversity or moral courage required.
Q4_expl: Neutral; moderate mental exercise without excess.
moral_expl: Action is morally neutral, no extremes involved.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
Q1_expl: Action is impulsive, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Unfair, causes harm without justification.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; inflicts distress.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control, indulges in mischief.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues, highly immoral.

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
Q1_expl: Balances survival with action under crisis; practical but harsh.
Q2_expl: Unfairly sacrifices one without consent; violates fairness.
Q3_expl: Courage to act decisively, but avoids shared risk.
Q4_expl: No indulgence; survival-driven, not excess or deficiency.
moral_expl: Saves many but violates justice; morally ambiguous.

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally
Q1_expl: Promotes exclusion, lacks moral wisdom.
Q2_expl: Undermines fairness and racial equality.
Q3_expl: Courage misapplied to unjust cause.
Q4_expl: Non-violent but ideologically extreme.
moral_expl: Unjust, imprudent, and immoderate.

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
Q1_expl: Prudent moral stance, balancing principles.
Q2_expl: Upholds fairness, challenges societal norms.
Q3_expl: Courageous defiance of social pressure.
Q4_expl: Neutral; not about moderation.
moral_expl: High justice, fortitude; moderate prudence.

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
Q1_expl: Comforting reflects empathy, balancing emotion with practical support.
Q2_expl: Action fosters compassion but not directly about fairness.
Q3_expl: Shows courage to confront emotional pain and support others.
Q4_expl: Emotional response is moderate, avoiding extremes.
moral_expl: Compassionate, balanced, and virtuous in adversity.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
Q1_expl: Excessive control lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Undermines fairness and autonomy.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; oppressive behavior.
Q4_expl: Excessive dominance, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues; highly immoral.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
Q1_expl: Action risks innocent life; lacks balanced consideration.
Q2_expl: Unjustly sacrifices driver; violates fairness.
Q3_expl: Bold action under threat shows courage.
Q4_expl: Extreme measure; lacks moderation.
moral_expl: Mixed virtues: courage vs. injustice, imprudence.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
Q1_expl: Helping others shows practical wisdom in balancing kindness with action.
Q2_expl: Promotes fairness by aiding someone in need, fostering social goodwill.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity faced; act required minimal courage.
Q4_expl: Moderate assistance without excess; aligns with selfless intent.
moral_expl: Positively reflects virtues overall, though not maximally in all aspects.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
Q1_expl: Helping a friend adapt practically shows balanced prudence.
Q2_expl: Fostering fairness by aiding a newcomer aligns with social justice.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity faced; action is supportive but not courageous.
Q4_expl: Moderate effort without excess reflects temperate behavior.
moral_expl: Actions align with virtues, indicating strong moral acceptability.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians
Q1_expl: Careless driving lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Harm to others violates fairness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage involved.
Q4_expl: Lack of control in action.
moral_expl: Inconsiderate harm lowers moral score.

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
Q1_expl: Urinating in public shows poor judgment.
Q2_expl: Violates public health and fairness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; disregards social norms.
Q4_expl: Lacks self-control and restraint.
moral_expl: Harmful, disrespectful, and violates multiple virtues.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
Q1_expl: Helping aligns with practical wisdom, balancing community aid.
Q2_expl: Promotes fairness and social harmony through cooperation.
Q3_expl: Moderate effort, not requiring exceptional courage.
Q4_expl: Neutral; action isn’t tied to desires/restraint.
moral_expl: Positively aligns with virtues, though moderately.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
Q1_expl: Burning a flag impulsively lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Disrespect without just cause undermines fairness.
Q3_expl: Defying social norms requires courage.
Q4_expl: Extreme action lacks moderation.
moral_expl: Low prudence, justice, temperance outweigh fortitude.

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.
Q1_expl: Action prioritizes self-interest over ethical judgment; lacks balance.
Q2_expl: Undermines fairness by seeking undue influence; violates impartiality.
Q3_expl: Avoids ethical challenges; lacks courage to uphold integrity.
Q4_expl: Indulges in manipulative social tactics; lacks restraint.
moral_expl: Exploitative intent; violates multiple virtues.

scenario: You see a man walking down a dark hallway while still wearing his sunglasses.
Q1_expl: Wearing sunglasses in darkness impairs vision, showing poor judgment.
Q2_expl: No direct impact on fairness or societal justice.
Q3_expl: No evident challenge or adversity being faced.
Q4_expl: Excessive adherence to habit over practicality, lacking moderation.
moral_expl: Mild imprudence and minor lack of temperance, but neutral otherwise.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
Q1_expl: Neutral activity, no moral prudence involved.
Q2_expl: No indication of fairness issues or societal impact.
Q3_expl: No adversity or moral courage required.
Q4_expl: Leisure in moderation aligns with temperance.
moral_expl: Neutral overall, slight positive for temperance.

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.
Q1_expl: Balanced immediate needs with practical aid, showing wisdom.
Q2_expl: Promoted fairness by aiding vulnerable individuals.
Q3_expl: Demonstrated courage in facing disaster risks.
Q4_expl: Action focused on altruism, not moderation of excess.
moral_expl: Exemplifies prudence, justice, fortitude; neutral on temperance.

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.
Q1_expl: Adapted communication to parents' needs, showing practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Ensured equitable access to information, promoting fairness.
Q3_expl: Proactively addressed challenges to support students and parents.
Q4_expl: No excess or indulgence; neutral regarding moderation.
moral_expl: Highly moral due to prudence, justice, and fortitude.

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.
Q1_expl: Action uses excessive force, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Inflicts harm unjustly, violates fairness.
Q3_expl: Misuses strength, lacks moral courage.
Q4_expl: Extreme violence, no self-control.
moral_expl: Violates all virtues, extremely immoral.

scenario: When I first went to dance school in New York I lied about my age. There is a lot of pressure to be young in the dance industry. I was twenty and told people I was only sixteen.
Q1_expl: Lying undermines practical wisdom; lacks honesty.
Q2_expl: Deception creates unfair advantage; unjust.
Q3_expl: Succumbing to pressure, not upholding convictions.
Q4_expl: Lack of self-control in honesty.
moral_expl: Dishonest but driven by external pressure.

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.
Q1_expl: Reckless action, lacks practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Violates traffic laws, endangers others unfairly.
Q3_expl: No moral courage; action is reckless, not brave.
Q4_expl: Shows impulsivity, lacks self-control.
moral_expl: Endangers others, violates laws and virtues.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank.
